title: Voice-Controlled Home Automation Hub
description: Offline voice recognition controller switching mains loads (lights, fans, pumps) via solid-state relays, with MQTT status reporting and BLE fallback app control. No cloud dependency.
objective: Recognise 10 voice commands offline; ≤ 500 ms response; control ≥ 4 mains channels; ≥ 30 operations/day.
constraints: 230 V; DIN-rail mount; no cloud dependency; CE-compliant; ≤ £45 BOM.
supporting_fact_titles: MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data
Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) for App Control
Voice Recognition — Keyword Spotting UART Module (LD3320)
N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch

element_usage_notes.Voice Recognition UART Speech Module: LD3320 UART @ 9600 baud; 20-word vocabulary; 1-shot user retrain supported.
element_usage_notes.Solid-State Relay Mains Actuator Switchi: 4-channel SSR board; 230 V / 10 A per channel; zero-cross switching.
element_usage_notes.ESP32 WiFi + MQTT IoT Telemetry: Broker on local network (192.168.1.x); topic home/automation/{channel}/state; QoS 1.
element_usage_notes.BLE GATT Smartphone App Interface: Fallback manual control; custom GATT service UUID; on/off per channel via BLE write.
element_usage_notes.MQTT QoS-1 Secure Event Logger: Logs every relay state change with timestamp; retained messages for last-state recovery on reboot.

element_interactions.description: LD3320 fires a UART byte on keyword recognition. ESP32 maps command_id to channel+action and dispatches to relay and MQTT logger.
App write characteristic triggers same dispatch path as voice commands — ensures BLE and voice share identical relay logic.
ESP32 asserts GPIO 16/17/18/19 to drive the four SSR control inputs. GPIO HIGH = SSR on; channel state cached in NVS for reboot recovery.
Every relay transition publishes a retained QoS-1 message. Logger aggregates to home/automation/log for audit and HA dashboard.
